seoTitle: How to Add Custom Fields to a Jira Issue Screen
metaDescription: Learn how to create a custom field in Jira and add it to the right issue screen so your team can capture the extra data they need.
intro: Jira's default fields don't cover every piece of information a team might need to track on an issue. This guide covers creating a custom field and adding it to the appropriate issue screen so it shows up where you need it.

steps.heading: Create a new custom field
steps.body: Go to Jira's field management settings and create a new custom field, choosing the field type that fits your data, such as text, dropdown, or date.

steps.heading: Configure the field's options
steps.body: If you're creating a dropdown or checkbox field, define the available options it should offer.

steps.heading: Associate the field with a screen
steps.body: Add the new custom field to the relevant issue screen, such as the create, edit, or view screen for your project.

steps.heading: Assign the screen to the right issue types
steps.body: Make sure the screen you edited is actually used by the issue types where you want this field to appear.

steps.heading: Test the field on an issue
steps.body: Open or create an issue using the updated screen to confirm the custom field appears and behaves as expected.

faq.question: Do I need admin permissions to add a custom field?
faq.answer: Yes, creating and configuring custom fields typically requires Jira administrator access.

faq.question: Can a custom field be required?
faq.answer: Yes, Jira lets you mark a field as required on a given screen, so users can't submit the issue without filling it in.

faq.question: Why isn't my new custom field showing up on issues?
faq.answer: This usually happens when the field hasn't been added to the correct screen, or the screen isn't associated with the issue type you're using.

faq.question: Can the same custom field be used across multiple projects?
faq.answer: Yes, once created, a custom field can be added to screens across different projects rather than being limited to just one.
